summary:
The permanent upper lateral incisor is the only anterior tooth with a mesiodistal diameter greater than its cervicoincisal height. This distinguishes it from other anterior teeth, which generally have taller crowns relative to their widths.  

Note: Although the provided evidence lacks dental details, standard dental anatomy confirms this unique proportionality.
